title: Add Coin Values to Find Total Cents
description: Add the values of bills and coins to find the total amount in cents.
condition: Given multiple coin and/or bill values in cents that represent one amount.
response: Add the values to compute the total number of cents.
label: Principle / Model

title: Compare Quantities by Subtraction
description: Subtract one quantity from another to determine how much more or less.
condition: Given two quantities and asked how much more, less, taller, longer, or fewer.
response: Subtract the smaller quantity from the larger to find the difference.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Used for length and count comparisons.

title: Find Amount to Next 100
description: Determine how many more are needed to reach the next hundred.
condition: Given a number less than 100 and asked how many more to make 100.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Often applied in money contexts with cents.

title: Find Missing Part from Total
description: Given a total and one part, subtract to find the missing part.
condition: Given total amount and one known part.
response: Subtract the known part from the total to find the unknown part.
label: Principle / Model

title: Identify Number Line Interval
description: Determine the interval between consecutive tick marks on a number line.
condition: Given a labeled number line with evenly spaced tick marks.
response: Find the difference between adjacent labeled values.
label: Concept

title: Locate Number on Number Line
description: Place or identify a number at the correct position on a number line.
condition: Given a number line and a target number.
response: Mark the position corresponding to that number.
label: Production / Skill

title: Measure Length in Inches
description: Determine the length of an object using inch markings.
condition: Given a picture of an object aligned with inch marks.
response: Count the number of inches to state the object's length.
label: Production / Skill
example: The pencil spans 5 inch marks, so it is 5 in.

title: Represent Amount with Fewest Bills and Coins
description: Use the largest possible denominations to represent a given value with the fewest pieces.
condition: Given a total amount of money and asked to show it using the fewest bills and coins.
response: Select the largest denominations first and combine to equal the total.
label: Principle / Model
notes: Applies place value and coin value knowledge.

title: Select Appropriate Measurement Unit
description: Choose the most reasonable unit (inch, foot, yard) based on the size of the object.
condition: Given an object and a list of possible measurement units.
response: Select the unit that best matches the object's length.
label: Concept
example: A bicycle is best measured in yards, not inches.
